{"result":"[{\"name_path\": \"ZWaveJSLock/async_get_usercodes\", \"kind\": \"Method\", \"relative_path\": \"custom_components/lock_code_manager/providers/zwave_js.py\", \"body_location\": {\"start_line\": 684, \"end_line\": 738}, \"body\": \"async def async_get_usercodes(self) -> dict[int, int | str]:\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"Get dictionary of code slots and usercodes.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        code_slots = {\\n            int(code_slot)\\n            for entry in self.hass.config_entries.async_entries(DOMAIN)\\n            for code_slot in get_entry_data(entry, CONF_SLOTS, {})\\n            if self.lock.entity_id in get_entry_data(entry, CONF_LOCKS, [])\\n        }\\n        data: dict[int, int | str] = {}\\n\\n        if not await self.async_is_connection_up():\\n            raise LockDisconnected\\n\\n        slots = self._get_usercodes_from_cache()\\n        slots_by_num = {int(slot[\\\"code_slot\\\"]): slot for slot in slots}\\n\\n        # If any configured slot is missing or has unknown state, do one hard\\n        # refresh to populate the cache. This is more efficient than fetching\\n        # individual slots and uses Z-Wave JS's checksum optimization.\\n        # Note: We call _async_refresh_usercode_cache directly here to avoid\\n        # recursion since async_hard_refresh_codes calls async_get_usercodes.\\n        if any(\\n            slot_num not in slots_by_num or slots_by_num[slot_num].get(\\\"in_use\\\") is None\\n            for slot_num in code_slots\\n        ):\\n            _LOGGER.debug(\\n                \\\"Lock %s has missing/unknown slots, performing hard refresh\\\",\\n                self.lock.entity_id,\\n            )\\n            await self._async_refresh_usercode_cache()\\n            slots = self._get_usercodes_from_cache()\\n            slots_by_num = {int(slot[\\\"code_slot\\\"]): slot for slot in slots}\\n\\n        for slot in slots:\\n            code_slot = int(slot[\\\"code_slot\\\"])\\n            usercode: str = slot[\\\"usercode\\\"] or \\\"\\\"\\n            in_use: bool | None = slot[\\\"in_use\\\"]\\n\\n            if not in_use:\\n                data[code_slot] = \\\"\\\"\\n            # Resolve PIN (handles masked codes), skip if unresolvable\\n            elif resolved := self._resolve_pin_if_masked(usercode, code_slot):\\n                data[code_slot] = resolved\\n\\n        slots_with_pin = [s for s, v in data.items() if v]\\n        slots_not_enabled = [s for s, v in data.items() if not v]\\n        _LOGGER.debug(\\n            \\\"Lock %s: %s slots with PIN %s, %s slots not enabled %s\\\",\\n            self.lock.entity_id,\\n            len(slots_with_pin),\\n            slots_with_pin,\\n            len(slots_not_enabled),\\n            slots_not_enabled,\\n        )\\n        return data\"}]"}

{"result":"[{\"name_path\": \"ZWaveJSLock/_async_handle_duplicate_code\", \"kind\": \"Method\", \"relative_path\": \"custom_components/lock_code_manager/providers/zwave_js.py\", \"body_location\": {\"start_line\": 478, \"end_line\": 539}, \"body\": \"async def _async_handle_duplicate_code(self) -> None:\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"Handle duplicate code rejection from the lock.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        lock_ent_id = self.lock.entity_id\\n        code_slot = self._set_in_progress_code_slot\\n        self._set_in_progress_code_slot = None\\n        if code_slot is None:\\n            return\\n        try:\\n            # Only move forward if a config entry manages this lock and code slot - there\\n            # can only be one due to our config entry uniqueness requirement.\\n            entry = next(\\n                entry\\n                for entry in self.hass.config_entries.async_entries(DOMAIN)\\n                if self.lock.entity_id in get_entry_data(entry, CONF_LOCKS, [])\\n                and code_slot in (int(s) for s in get_entry_data(entry, CONF_SLOTS, {}))\\n            )\\n        except StopIteration:\\n            return\\n\\n        # Guard that will never trigger but satisfies type checkers that an entity exists\\n        if (\\n            enabled_ent_id := self.ent_reg.async_get_entity_id(\\n                SWITCH_DOMAIN, DOMAIN, f\\\"{entry.entry_id}|{code_slot}|{CONF_ENABLED}\\\"\\n            )\\n        ) is None:\\n            return\\n\\n        await self.hass.services.async_call(\\n            SWITCH_DOMAIN,\\n            SERVICE_TURN_OFF,\\n            {ATTR_ENTITY_ID: enabled_ent_id},\\n            blocking=True,\\n        )\\n\\n        entry_title = entry.title or entry.entry_id\\n\\n        def _dupe_msg(\\n            lock_entity_id: str, code_slot: str, config_entry_title: str\\n        ) -> str:\\n            \\\"\\\"\\\"Return message for duplicate code rejection.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n            return (\\n                f\\\"Lock {lock_entity_id} rejected the code for slot {code_slot} \\\"\\n                f\\\"(config entry {config_entry_title}) because it duplicates a code \\\"\\n                f\\\"in another slot. Slot {code_slot} has been disabled. Please set a \\\"\\n                f\\\"unique code and re-enable slot {code_slot}\\\"\\n            )\\n\\n        _LOGGER.error(\\n            _dupe_msg(\\\"%s\\\", \\\"%s\\\", \\\"%s\\\"),\\n            lock_ent_id,\\n            code_slot,\\n            entry_title,\\n            code_slot,\\n            code_slot,\\n        )\\n\\n        async_create(\\n            self.hass,\\n            _dupe_msg(f\\\"**{lock_ent_id}**\\\", f\\\"**{code_slot}**\\\", f\\\"**{entry_title}**\\\"),\\n            title=\\\"Duplicate Lock Code Rejected\\\",\\n            notification_id=(f\\\"{DOMAIN}_{lock_ent_id}_{code_slot}_dupe\\\"),\\n        )\"}]"}

{"result":"[{\"name_path\": \"ZWaveJSLock/async_set_usercode\", \"kind\": \"Method\", \"relative_path\": \"custom_components/lock_code_manager/providers/zwave_js.py\", \"body_location\": {\"start_line\": 579, \"end_line\": 627}, \"body\": \"async def async_set_usercode(\\n        self, code_slot: int, usercode: int | str, name: str | None = None\\n    ) -> bool:\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        Set a usercode on a code slot.\\n\\n        Returns True if the value was changed, False if already set to this value.\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        # Check if the code is already set to this value (avoid unnecessary network call)\\n        usercode = str(usercode)\\n        try:\\n            if (current := get_usercode(self.node, code_slot)).get(\\\"in_use\\\"):\\n                current_code = str(current.get(\\\"usercode\\\", \\\"\\\"))\\n                # Resolve masked codes and compare\\n                if usercode == self._resolve_pin_if_masked(current_code, code_slot):\\n                    _LOGGER.debug(\\n                        \\\"Lock %s slot %s already has this PIN, skipping set\\\",\\n                        self.lock.entity_id,\\n                        code_slot,\\n                    )\\n                    return False\\n        except Exception:\\n            # If we can't check the cache, proceed with the set\\n            pass\\n\\n        self._set_in_progress_code_slot = code_slot\\n        service_data = {\\n            ATTR_ENTITY_ID: self.lock.entity_id,\\n            ATTR_CODE_SLOT: code_slot,\\n            ATTR_USERCODE: usercode,\\n        }\\n        await self.async_call_service(\\n            ZWAVE_JS_DOMAIN, SERVICE_SET_LOCK_USERCODE, service_data\\n        )\\n        # V1 locks don't reliably update the Z-Wave JS value cache after set.\\n        # Poll the slot directly from the device to force-update the cache\\n        # before the coordinator reads it, preventing sync loops.\\n        # No try-except: if the poll fails, we must not proceed with the\\n        # optimistic update since async_request_refresh() would overwrite it\\n        # with stale cache data, reintroducing the sync loop. Letting the\\n        # error propagate allows the sync mechanism to retry the operation.\\n        if self._usercode_cc_version < 2:\\n            await get_usercode_from_node(self.node, code_slot)\\n        # Optimistic update: Z-Wave command succeeded (lock acknowledged), but the\\n        # value cache updates asynchronously via push notification. Update coordinator\\n        # immediately to prevent sync loops from reading stale cache data.\\n        if self.coordinator:\\n            self.coordinator.push_update({code_slot: usercode})\\n        return True\"}]"}

{"result":"[{\"name_path\": \"ZWaveJSLock/_handle_zwave_js_event\", \"kind\": \"Method\", \"relative_path\": \"custom_components/lock_code_manager/providers/zwave_js.py\", \"body_location\": {\"start_line\": 436, \"end_line\": 476}, \"body\": \"@callback\\n    def _handle_zwave_js_event(self, evt: Event) -> None:\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"Handle Z-Wave JS event.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        if evt.data[ATTR_TYPE] != NotificationType.ACCESS_CONTROL:\\n            _LOGGER.debug(\\n                \\\"Lock %s received non Access Control event: %s\\\",\\n                self.lock.entity_id,\\n                evt.as_dict(),\\n            )\\n            return\\n\\n        params = evt.data.get(ATTR_PARAMETERS) or {}\\n        code_slot = params.get(\\\"userId\\\", 0)\\n\\n        # Handle duplicate code rejection — only when LCM initiated the set\\n        if (\\n            evt.data[ATTR_EVENT]\\n            == AccessControlNotificationEvent.NEW_USER_CODE_NOT_ADDED_DUE_TO_DUPLICATE_CODE\\n            and self._set_in_progress_code_slot is not None\\n            and code_slot in (0, self._set_in_progress_code_slot)\\n        ):\\n            slot = self._set_in_progress_code_slot or code_slot\\n            self.hass.async_create_task(\\n                self._async_handle_duplicate_code(),\\n                f\\\"Handle duplicate code for {self.lock.entity_id} slot {slot}\\\",\\n            )\\n            return\\n\\n        self.async_fire_code_slot_event(\\n            code_slot=code_slot,\\n            to_locked=next(\\n                (\\n                    to_locked\\n                    for to_locked, codes in ACCESS_CONTROL_NOTIFICATION_TO_LOCKED.items()\\n                    if evt.data[ATTR_EVENT] in codes\\n                ),\\n                None,\\n            ),\\n            action_text=evt.data.get(ATTR_EVENT_LABEL),\\n            source_data=evt,\\n        )\"}]"}

{"result":"[{\"name_path\": \"ZWaveJSLock/subscribe_push_updates\", \"kind\": \"Method\", \"relative_path\": \"custom_components/lock_code_manager/providers/zwave_js.py\", \"body_location\": {\"start_line\": 272, \"end_line\": 389}, \"body\": \"@callback\\n    def subscribe_push_updates(self) -> None:\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"Subscribe to User Code CC value update events.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        # Idempotent - skip if already subscribed\\n        if self._value_update_unsub is not None:\\n            return\\n\\n        ready, reason = self._get_client_state()\\n        if not ready:\\n            _LOGGER.debug(\\n                \\\"Lock %s: push subscription deferred (%s)\\\",\\n                self.lock.entity_id,\\n                reason,\\n            )\\n            self._schedule_push_retry(reason)\\n            return\\n\\n        @callback\\n        def on_value_updated(event: dict[str, Any]) -> None:\\n            \\\"\\\"\\\"Handle value update events from Z-Wave JS.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n            args: dict[str, Any] = event[\\\"args\\\"]\\n            # Filter for User Code CC - handle both userCode and userIdStatus\\n            if args.get(\\\"commandClass\\\") != CommandClass.USER_CODE:\\n                return\\n\\n            property_name = args.get(\\\"property\\\")\\n            if property_name not in (\\n                LOCK_USERCODE_PROPERTY,\\n                LOCK_USERCODE_STATUS_PROPERTY,\\n            ):\\n                return\\n\\n            code_slot = int(args[\\\"propertyKey\\\"])\\n\\n            # Slot 0 is not a valid user code slot (used for status/metadata)\\n            if code_slot == 0:\\n                return\\n\\n            # Clear in-progress tracking only on userCode updates for the slot\\n            # we were setting. userIdStatus updates don't confirm acceptance and\\n            # could race with duplicate-code notifications.\\n            if (\\n                property_name == LOCK_USERCODE_PROPERTY\\n                and code_slot == self._set_in_progress_code_slot\\n            ):\\n                self._set_in_progress_code_slot = None\\n\\n            # Handle userIdStatus updates - slot cleared is a fast path\\n            if property_name == LOCK_USERCODE_STATUS_PROPERTY:\\n                status = args.get(\\\"newValue\\\")\\n                if status == CodeSlotStatus.AVAILABLE:\\n                    # Ignore AVAILABLE status if LCM expects a PIN on this slot.\\n                    # Some locks send stale AVAILABLE events after a code was set,\\n                    # which would cause infinite sync loops.\\n                    if self._slot_expects_pin(code_slot):\\n                        _LOGGER.debug(\\n                            \\\"Lock %s: ignoring userIdStatus=AVAILABLE for slot %s \\\"\\n                            \\\"(LCM expects PIN on this slot)\\\",\\n                            self.lock.entity_id,\\n                            code_slot,\\n                        )\\n                        return\\n\\n                    # Slot was cleared - update coordinator if needed\\n                    if self.coordinator and self.coordinator.data.get(code_slot) != \\\"\\\":\\n                        _LOGGER.debug(\\n                            \\\"Lock %s: slot %s userIdStatus=AVAILABLE, marking cleared\\\",\\n                            self.lock.entity_id,\\n                            code_slot,\\n                        )\\n                        self.coordinator.push_update({code_slot: \\\"\\\"})\\n                return\\n\\n            # Handle userCode updates\\n            # newValue is the raw PIN string (or None/empty/\\\"0000\\\" if cleared)\\n            new_value = args.get(\\\"newValue\\\")\\n            # Treat empty, None, or all-zeros as cleared\\n            if not new_value or (\\n                isinstance(new_value, str) and new_value.strip(\\\"0\\\") == \\\"\\\"\\n            ):\\n                value = \\\"\\\"\\n            else:\\n                value = str(new_value)\\n\\n            # Resolve masked codes (all asterisks) to expected PIN\\n            # This prevents infinite sync loops when locks return masked values\\n            resolved = self._resolve_pin_if_masked(value, code_slot)\\n            if resolved is None:\\n                _LOGGER.debug(\\n                    \\\"Lock %s: skipping masked code update for slot %s \\\"\\n                    \\\"(unable to resolve)\\\",\\n                    self.lock.entity_id,\\n                    code_slot,\\n                )\\n                return\\n\\n            # Skip if value hasn't changed (Z-Wave JS sends duplicate events)\\n            if self.coordinator and self.coordinator.data.get(code_slot) == resolved:\\n                return\\n\\n            _LOGGER.debug(\\n                \\\"Lock %s received push update for slot %s: %s\\\",\\n                self.lock.entity_id,\\n                code_slot,\\n                \\\"****\\\" if resolved else \\\"(cleared)\\\",\\n            )\\n\\n            # Push update to coordinator\\n            if self.coordinator:\\n                self.coordinator.push_update({code_slot: resolved})\\n\\n        try:\\n            self._value_update_unsub = self.node.on(\\\"value updated\\\", on_value_updated)\\n        except ValueError as err:\\n            _LOGGER.debug(\\n                \\\"Lock %s push subscription deferred: %s\\\", self.lock.entity_id, err\\n            )\\n            self._schedule_push_retry(\\\"node not ready\\\")\"}]"}

{"result":"[{\"name_path\": \"LockUsercodeUpdateCoordinator/async_get_usercodes\", \"kind\": \"Method\", \"relative_path\": \"custom_components/lock_code_manager/coordinator.py\", \"body_location\": {\"start_line\": 78, \"end_line\": 86}, \"body\": \"async def async_get_usercodes(self) -> dict[int, int | str]:\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"Update usercodes.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        try:\\n            return await self._lock.async_internal_get_usercodes()\\n        except LockCodeManagerError as err:\\n            # We can silently fail if we've never been able to retrieve data\\n            if not self.last_update_success:\\n                return {}\\n            raise UpdateFailed from err\"}]"}

{"result":"[{\"name_path\": \"LockUsercodeUpdateCoordinator/push_update\", \"kind\": \"Method\", \"relative_path\": \"custom_components/lock_code_manager/coordinator.py\", \"body_location\": {\"start_line\": 64, \"end_line\": 76}, \"body\": \"@callback\\n    def push_update(self, updates: dict[int, int | str]) -> None:\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"Push one or more slot updates and notify listening entities.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        if not updates:\\n            return\\n\\n        new_data = {**self.data, **updates}\\n        # Skip update if data hasn't actually changed to avoid redundant logging\\n        # and unnecessary listener notifications\\n        if new_data == self.data:\\n            return\\n\\n        self.async_set_updated_data(new_data)\"}]"}

Identify changes that need to be made\\n    slots_to_add: dict[int, Any] = {\\n        k: v for k, v in new_slots.items() if k not in curr_slots\\n    }\\n    slots_to_remove: dict[int, Any] = {\\n        k: v for k, v in curr_slots.items() if k not in new_slots\\n    }\\n    locks_to_add: list[str] = [lock for lock in new_locks if lock not in curr_locks]\\n    locks_to_remove: list[str] = [lock for lock in curr_locks if lock not in new_locks]\\n\\n    callbacks = runtime_data.callbacks\\n\\n    # Remove old lock entities (slot sensors)\\n    for lock_entity_id in locks_to_remove:\\n        _LOGGER.debug(\\n            \\\"%s (%s): Removing lock %s entities\\\", entry_id, entry_title, lock_entity_id\\n        )\\n        callbacks.invoke_lock_removed_handlers(lock_entity_id)\\n        lock: BaseLock = hass.data[DOMAIN][CONF_LOCKS][lock_entity_id]\\n        if lock.device_entry:\\n            dev_reg = dr.async_get(hass)\\n            dev_reg.async_update_device(\\n                lock.device_entry.id, remove_config_entry_id=entry_id\\n            )\\n        await async_unload_lock(\\n            hass, config_entry, lock_entity_id=lock_entity_id, remove_permanently=True\\n        )\\n\\n    # Notify any existing entities that additional locks have been added then create\\n    # slot PIN sensors for the new locks\\n    if locks_to_add:\\n        _LOGGER.debug(\\n            \\\"%s (%s): Adding following locks: %s\\\",\\n            entry_id,\\n            entry_title,\\n            locks_to_add,\\n        )\\n        added_locks: list[BaseLock] = []\\n        for lock_entity_id in locks_to_add:\\n            if lock_entity_id in hass_data[CONF_LOCKS]:\\n                _LOGGER.debug(\\n                    \\\"%s (%s): Reusing lock instance for lock %s\\\",\\n                    entry_id,\\n                    entry_title,\\n                    hass_data[CONF_LOCKS][lock_entity_id],\\n                )\\n                lock = runtime_data.locks[lock_entity_id] = hass_data[CONF_LOCKS][\\n                    lock_entity_id\\n                ]\\n                await lock.async_wait_for_setup()\\n            else:\\n                lock = hass_data[CONF_LOCKS][lock_entity_id] = runtime_data.locks[\\n                    lock_entity_id\\n                ] = async_create_lock_instance(\\n                    hass,\\n                    dr.async_get(hass),\\n                    ent_reg,\\n                    config_entry,\\n                    lock_entity_id,\\n                )\\n                _LOGGER.debug(\\n                    \\\"%s (%s): Creating lock instance for lock %s\\\",\\n                    entry_id,\\n                    entry_title,\\n                    lock,\\n                )\\n                await lock.async_setup_internal(config_entry)\\n\\n            added_locks.append(lock)\\n\\n            # Check if lock is connected (but don't wait - entity creation doesn't require it)\\n            if not await lock.async_internal_is_connection_up():\\n                _LOGGER.debug(\\n                    \\\"%s (%s): Lock %s is not connected yet. Entities will be created \\\"\\n                    \\\"but will be unavailable until the lock comes online. This is normal \\\"\\n                    \\\"during startup if Z-Wave JS is still initializing.\\\",\\n                    entry_id,\\n                    entry_title,\\n                    lock.lock.entity_id,\\n                )\\n\\n            for slot_num in new_slots:\\n                _LOGGER.debug(\\n                    \\\"%s (%s): Adding lock %s slot %s sensor and event entity\\\",\\n                    entry_id,\\n                    entry_title,\\n                    lock_entity_id,\\n                    slot_num,\\n                )\\n                callbacks.invoke_lock_slot_adders(lock, slot_num, ent_reg)\\n\\n        # Notify existing entities about the new locks\\n        if added_locks:\\n            callbacks.invoke_lock_added_handlers(added_locks)\\n\\n    # Remove slot sensors that are no longer in the config\\n    for slot_num in slots_to_remove.keys():\\n        _LOGGER.debug(\\n            \\\"%s (%s): Removing slot %s sensors\\\", entry_id, entry_title, slot_num\\n        )\\n        await callbacks.invoke_entity_removers_for_slot(slot_num)\\n\\n    # For each new slot, add standard entities and configuration entities. We also\\n    # add slot sensors for existing locks only since new locks were already set up\\n    # above.\\n    for slot_num, slot_config in slots_to_add.items():\\n        entities_to_remove.clear()\\n        # First we store the set of entities we are adding so we can track when they are\\n        # done\\n        entities_to_add = {\\n            CONF_ENABLED,\\n            CONF_NAME,\\n            CONF_PIN,\\n            EVENT_PIN_USED,\\n        }\\n\\n        # Check if we need to add a number of uses entity\\n        if slot_config.get(CONF_NUMBER_OF_USES) not in (None, \\\"\\\"):\\n            entities_to_add.add(CONF_NUMBER_OF_USES)\\n\\n        _LOGGER.debug(\\n            \\\"%s (%s): Adding PIN enabled binary sensor for slot %s\\\",\\n            entry_id,\\n            entry_title,\\n            slot_num,\\n        )\\n        callbacks.invoke_standard_adders(slot_num, ent_reg)\\n        for key in entities_to_add:\\n            _LOGGER.debug(\\n                \\\"%s (%s): Adding %s entity for slot %s\\\",\\n                entry_id,\\n                entry_title,\\n                key,\\n                slot_num,\\n            )\\n            if key in callbacks.add_keyed_entity:\\n                callbacks.invoke_keyed_adders(key, slot_num, ent_reg)\\n\\n        for lock_entity_id, lock in runtime_data.locks.items():\\n            if lock_entity_id in locks_to_add:\\n                continue\\n            _LOGGER.debug(\\n                \\\"%s (%s): Adding lock %s slot %s sensor\\\",\\n                entry_id,\\n                entry_title,\\n                lock_entity_id,\\n                slot_num,\\n            )\\n            callbacks.invoke_lock_slot_adders(lock, slot_num, ent_reg)\\n\\n    # For all slots that are in both the old and new config, check if any of the\\n    # configuration options have changed\\n    for slot_num in set(curr_slots).intersection(new_slots):\\n        entities_to_remove.clear()\\n        entities_to_add.clear()\\n        # Check if number of uses has changed\\n        old_val = curr_slots[slot_num].get(CONF_NUMBER_OF_USES)\\n        new_val = new_slots[slot_num].get(CONF_NUMBER_OF_USES)\\n\\n        # If number of uses value hasn't changed, skip\\n        if old_val == new_val:\\n            continue\\n\\n        # If number of uses value has been removed, fire a signal to remove\\n        # corresponding entity\\n        if old_val not in (None, \\\"\\\") and new_val in (None, \\\"\\\"):\\n            entities_to_remove.add(CONF_NUMBER_OF_USES)\\n        # If number of uses value has been added, fire a signal to add\\n        # corresponding entity\\n        elif old_val in (None, \\\"\\\") and new_val not in (None, \\\"\\\"):\\n            entities_to_add.add(CONF_NUMBER_OF_USES)\\n\\n        for key in entities_to_remove:\\n            _LOGGER.debug(\\n                \\\"%s (%s): Removing %s entity for slot %s due to changed configuration\\\",\\n                entry_id,\\n                entry_title,\\n                key,\\n                slot_num,\\n            )\\n            await callbacks.invoke_entity_removers_for_key(slot_num, key)\\n\\n        for key in entities_to_add:\\n            _LOGGER.debug(\\n                \\\"%s (%s): Adding %s entity for slot %s due to changed configuration\\\",\\n                entry_id,\\n                entry_title,\\n                key,\\n                slot_num,\\n            )\\n            callbacks.invoke_keyed_adders(key, slot_num, ent_reg)\\n\\n    # Existing entities will listen to updates and act on it\\n    new_data = {CONF_LOCKS: new_locks, CONF_SLOTS: new_slots}\\n    _LOGGER.info(\\n        \\\"%s (%s): Done creating and/or updating entities\\\", entry_id, entry_title\\n    )\\n    hass.config_entries.async_update_entry(config_entry, data=new_data, options={})\"}]"}

   120→    _subscribed_condition_entity_id: str | None = None

   122→    @property

   124→        """Return condition entity ID for this slot."""
   125→        return get_slot_data(self.config_entry, self.slot_num).get(CONF_ENTITY_ID)
   126→
   127→    @callback
   128→    def _cleanup_condition_subscription(self) -> None:
   129→        """Clean up condition entity subscription if one exists."""
   130→        if self._condition_entity_unsub:
   131→            self._condition_entity_unsub()
   132→            self._condition_entity_unsub = None
   133→
   134→    @callback
   135→    def _update_state(self, _: datetime | None = None) -> None:
   136→        """Update binary sensor state by getting dependent states."""
   137→        _LOGGER.debug(
   138→            "%s (%s): Updating %s",
   139→            self.config_entry.entry_id,
   140→            self.config_entry.title,
   141→            self.entity_id,
   142→        )
   143→
   144→        states: dict[str, bool | None] = {}
   145→        for key, state in get_slot_data(self.config_entry, self.slot_num).items():
   146→            if key in (EVENT_PIN_USED, CONF_NAME, CONF_PIN, ATTR_IN_SYNC):
   147→                continue
   148→
   149→            # Handle condition entity - ON means access granted
   150→            if key == CONF_ENTITY_ID and (hass_state := self.hass.states.get(state)):
   151→                states[key] = (
   152→                    hass_state.state == STATE_ON
   153→                    if hass_state.state in (STATE_ON, STATE_OFF)
   154→                    else None
   155→                )
   156→                continue
   157→
   158→            if key == CONF_NUMBER_OF_USES:
   159→                states[key] = bool(int(float(state)))
   160→                continue
   161→            states[key] = state
   162→

   164→        # of uses, greater than 0
   165→        inactive_because_of = [key for key, state in states.items() if not state]
   166→        self._attr_is_on = bool(not inactive_because_of)
   167→        if inactive_because_of:
   168→            self._attr_extra_state_attributes["inactive_because_of"] = (
   169→                inactive_because_of
   170→            )
   171→        else:
   172→            self._attr_extra_state_attributes.pop("inactive_because_of", None)
   173→        self.async_write_ha_state()

   175→    async def _config_entry_update_listener(
   176→        self, hass: HomeAssistant, config_entry: LockCodeManagerConfigEntry
   177→    ) -> None:
   178→        """Update listener."""
   179→        if config_entry.options:
   180→            return
   181→        # Re-subscribe if condition entity changed
   182→        self._update_condition_entity_subscription()
   183→        self._update_state()
   184→
   185→    @callback
   186→    def _handle_condition_entity_state_change(
   187→        self, event: Event[EventStateChangedData]
   188→    ) -> None:
   189→        """Handle condition entity state change."""
   190→        self._update_state()
   191→
   192→    @callback
   193→    def _update_condition_entity_subscription(self) -> None:
   194→        """Update subscription for condition entity if it changed."""
   195→        current_entity_id = self._condition_entity_id
   196→        old_entity_id = self._subscribed_condition_entity_id
   197→
   198→        # No change needed if entity ID hasn't changed
   199→        if current_entity_id == old_entity_id:
   200→            return
   201→
   202→        # Unsubscribe from old entity if we had one
   203→        self._cleanup_condition_subscription()

   205→        # Subscribe to new entity if we have one

   210→                self._handle_condition_entity_state_change,
   211→            )
   212→
   213→        self._subscribed_condition_entity_id = current_entity_id
   214→        _LOGGER.debug(
   215→            "%s (%s): Updated condition entity subscription for %s: %s -> %s",
   216→            self.config_entry.entry_id,
   217→            self.config_entry.title,
   218→            self.entity_id,
   219→            old_entity_id,
   220→            current_entity_id,
   221→        )

   236→        )
   237→

   263→        CoordinatorEntity.__init__(self, coordinator)
   264→        self._entity_id_map: dict[str, str] = {}
   265→        self._active_unique_id = self._get_uid(ATTR_ACTIVE)
   266→        self._name_text_unique_id = self._get_uid(CONF_NAME)
   267→        self._pin_text_unique_id = self._get_uid(CONF_PIN)

   270→            f"{self._get_uid(ATTR_CODE)}|{lock_entity_id}"
   271→        )
   272→        self._lock = asyncio.Lock()
   273→        self._attr_is_on: bool | None = None  # None means not yet initialized
   274→        self._tracked_entity_ids: set[str] = set()
   275→        self._retry_unsub: Callable[[], None] | None = None
   276→        self._retry_active = False
   277→        self._state_tracking_unsub: Callable[[], None] | None = None
   278→        self._tracking_all_states: bool = False
   279→
   280→    @property
   281→    def should_poll(self) -> bool:
   282→        """Return whether entity should poll."""
   283→        return True
   284→
   285→    @property
   286→    def available(self) -> bool:
   287→        """Return whether binary sensor is available or not."""
   288→        return BaseLockCodeManagerCodeSlotPerLockEntity._is_available(self) and (
   289→            int(self.slot_num) in self.coordinator.data

   291→
   292→    async def async_update(self) -> None:
   293→        """Update entity."""
   294→        if self._attr_is_on is None:
   295→            return
   296→
   297→        if (
   298→            self._lock.locked()
   299→            or self.is_on
   300→            or not (state := self.hass.states.get(self.lock.lock.entity_id))
   301→            or state.state in (STATE_UNAVAILABLE, STATE_UNKNOWN)
   302→            or (not self.coordinator.last_update_success and not self._retry_active)
   303→        ):

   306→        _LOGGER.debug(
   307→            "Code slot %s on %s is out of sync, syncing now",
   308→            self.slot_num,

   314→        """Get entity state."""
   315→        if (state := self.hass.states.get(self._entity_id_map[key])) is None:

   319→    def _update_sync_state(self, is_on: bool) -> None:
   320→        """Update sync state and write to Home Assistant."""

   322→        self.async_write_ha_state()
   323→
   324→    @callback
   325→    def _handle_coordinator_update(self) -> None:
   326→        """
   327→        Handle updated data from the coordinator.
   328→
   329→        Triggers sync check when coordinator data changes to ensure we sync
   330→        when the lock reports different codes than expected (e.g., someone
   331→        manually changed a code on the lock).
   332→
   333→        Multiple tasks may be queued during rapid updates, but they serialize
   334→        via asyncio.Lock and each reads the latest coordinator data when it runs.
   335→        After the first successful sync, subsequent tasks exit early (is_on=True).
   336→        """
   337→        super()._handle_coordinator_update()
   338→        # Skip if not yet initialized or sync already in progress
   339→        if self._attr_is_on is not None and not self._lock.locked():
   340→            self.hass.async_create_task(
   341→                self._async_update_state(),
   342→                name=f"lcm_sync_check_{self.entity_id}",
   343→            )
   344→
   345→    def _cancel_retry(self) -> None:
   346→        """Cancel any scheduled retry callback."""
   347→        if self._retry_unsub:
   348→            self._retry_unsub()
   349→            self._retry_unsub = None
   350→        self._retry_active = False
   351→
   352→    def _schedule_retry(self) -> None:
   353→        """Schedule a retry if one isn't already pending."""
   354→        if self._retry_unsub:
   355→            return
   356→
   357→        _LOGGER.debug(
   358→            "%s (%s): Scheduling retry for %s slot %s in %ss",
   359→            self.config_entry.entry_id,
   360→            self.config_entry.title,
   361→            self.lock.lock.entity_id,
   362→            self.slot_num,
   363→            RETRY_DELAY.total_seconds(),
   364→        )
   365→
   366→        self._retry_unsub = async_call_later(
   367→            self.hass,
   368→            RETRY_DELAY.total_seconds(),
   369→            self._handle_retry_callback,
   370→        )
   371→
   372→    async def _handle_retry_callback(self, _now: datetime) -> None:
   373→        """Handle retry callback."""
   374→        if self._retry_unsub:
   375→            self._retry_unsub()
   376→            self._retry_unsub = None
   377→        self._retry_active = True
   378→        try:
   379→            await self.async_update()
   380→        finally:
   381→            self._retry_active = False
   382→
   383→    def _build_entity_id_map(self) -> bool:
   384→        """Build and cache entity IDs for this slot."""
   385→        missing = False
   386→        for key, domain, unique_id in (
   387→            (CONF_PIN, TEXT_DOMAIN, self._pin_text_unique_id),
   388→            (CONF_NAME, TEXT_DOMAIN, self._name_text_unique_id),
   389→            (ATTR_ACTIVE, BINARY_SENSOR_DOMAIN, self._active_unique_id),
   390→            (ATTR_CODE, SENSOR_DOMAIN, self._lock_slot_sensor_unique_id),
   391→        ):
   392→            if key in self._entity_id_map:
   393→                continue
   394→            ent_id = self.ent_reg.async_get_entity_id(domain, DOMAIN, unique_id)
   395→            if not ent_id:
   396→                missing = True
   397→                continue
   398→            self._entity_id_map[key] = ent_id
   399→        self._tracked_entity_ids = set(self._entity_id_map.values())
   400→        return not missing
   401→
   402→    def _ensure_entities_ready(self) -> bool:
   403→        """Ensure all dependent entities exist with valid states."""
   404→        for key in (CONF_PIN, CONF_NAME, ATTR_ACTIVE, ATTR_CODE):
   405→            if key not in self._entity_id_map:
   406→                return False
   407→
   408→            # Verify entity has a state
   409→            state = self._get_entity_state(key)
   410→            if state is None or state in (STATE_UNAVAILABLE, STATE_UNKNOWN):
   411→                _LOGGER.debug(
   412→                    "%s (%s): Waiting for %s state for %s slot %s",
   413→                    self.config_entry.entry_id,
   414→                    self.config_entry.title,
   415→                    key,
   416→                    self.lock.lock.entity_id,
   417→                    self.slot_num,
   418→                )
   419→                return False
   420→
   421→        return True
   422→
   423→    def _calculate_expected_sync(self, slot_state: _SlotState) -> bool:
   424→        """
   425→        Calculate whether slot should be in sync.
   426→
   427→        Active: PIN should match code on lock
   428→        Inactive: Code on lock should be empty
   429→        """
   430→        # Use coordinator data if available, otherwise fall back to sensor state
   431→        lock_code = (
   432→            str(slot_state.coordinator_code)
   433→            if slot_state.coordinator_code is not None
   434→            else slot_state.code_state
   435→        )
   436→        if slot_state.active_state == STATE_ON:
   437→            return slot_state.pin_state == lock_code
   438→        return lock_code == ""
   439→
   440→    @dataclass(frozen=True)
   441→    class _SlotState:
   442→        active_state: str
   443→        pin_state: str
   444→        name_state: str | None
   445→        code_state: str
   446→        coordinator_code: int | str | None
   447→
   448→    def _resolve_slot_state(
   449→        self, event: Event[EventStateChangedData] | None
   450→    ) -> _SlotState | None:
   451→        """Resolve slot state while applying shared guards."""
   452→        entity_id = event.data["entity_id"] if event else None
   453→        to_state = event.data["new_state"] if event else None
   454→
   455→        if not self.coordinator.last_update_success and not self._retry_active:
   456→            return None
   457→
   458→        if not self._build_entity_id_map():
   459→            return None
   460→
   461→        # NOTE: We intentionally don't call _update_state_tracking_filter() here.
   462→        # While it would be nice to switch from tracking all states to only specific
   463→        # entities, modifying subscriptions from within a callback causes timing issues.
   464→        # The performance impact of tracking all states is minimal since this method
   465→        # has early-return guards that skip processing for irrelevant entities.
   466→
   467→        if self._attr_is_on is None and int(self.slot_num) not in self.coordinator.data:
   468→            _LOGGER.debug(
   469→                "%s (%s): Slot %s not yet in coordinator data, skipping",
   470→                self.config_entry.entry_id,
   471→                self.config_entry.title,
   472→                self.slot_num,
   473→            )
   474→            return None
   475→
   476→        if entity_id is not None and entity_id not in self._tracked_entity_ids:
   477→            return None
   478→
   479→        if to_state and to_state.state in (STATE_UNAVAILABLE, STATE_UNKNOWN):
   480→            return None
   481→
   482→        if not self._ensure_entities_ready():
   483→            return None
   484→
   485→        active_state = self._get_entity_state(ATTR_ACTIVE)
   486→        pin_state = self._get_entity_state(CONF_PIN)
   487→        name_state = self._get_entity_state(CONF_NAME)
   488→        code_state = self._get_entity_state(ATTR_CODE)
   489→
   490→        if active_state is None or pin_state is None or code_state is None:
   491→            return None
   492→
   493→        coordinator_code = self.coordinator.data.get(int(self.slot_num))
   494→        return self._SlotState(
   495→            active_state=active_state,
   496→            pin_state=pin_state,
   497→            name_state=name_state,
   498→            code_state=code_state,
   499→            coordinator_code=coordinator_code,
   500→        )
   501→
   502→    async def _perform_sync_operation(self, slot_state: _SlotState) -> bool:
   503→        """
   504→        Perform sync operation (set or clear usercode).
   505→
   506→        Returns True if sync was performed, False if lock disconnected.
   507→        """
   508→        try:
   509→            if slot_state.active_state == STATE_ON:
   510→                await self.lock.async_internal_set_usercode(
   511→                    int(self.slot_num), slot_state.pin_state, slot_state.name_state
   512→                )
   513→                _LOGGER.debug(
   514→                    "%s (%s): Set usercode for %s slot %s",
   515→                    self.config_entry.entry_id,
   516→                    self.config_entry.title,
   517→                    self.lock.lock.entity_id,
   518→                    self.slot_num,
   519→                )
   520→            else:  # active_state == STATE_OFF
   521→                await self.lock.async_internal_clear_usercode(int(self.slot_num))
   522→                _LOGGER.debug(
   523→                    "%s (%s): Cleared usercode for %s slot %s",
   524→                    self.config_entry.entry_id,
   525→                    self.config_entry.title,
   526→                    self.lock.lock.entity_id,
   527→                    self.slot_num,
   528→                )
   529→            self._cancel_retry()
   530→            return True
   531→        except LockDisconnected as err:
   532→            _LOGGER.debug(
   533→                "%s (%s): Unable to %s usercode for %s slot %s: %s",
   534→                self.config_entry.entry_id,
   535→                self.config_entry.title,
   536→                "set" if slot_state.active_state == STATE_ON else "clear",
   537→                self.lock.lock.entity_id,
   538→                self.slot_num,
   539→                err,
   540→            )
   541→            self._schedule_retry()
   542→            return False
   543→
   544→    async def _async_update_state(
   545→        self, event: Event[EventStateChangedData] | None = None
   546→    ) -> None:
   547→        """
   548→        Update binary sensor state by checking dependent entity states.
   549→
   550→        On initial load (when _attr_is_on is None): Sets sync state without operations.
   551→        On subsequent updates: Performs sync operations when out of sync.
   552→        """
   553→        async with self._lock:
   554→            slot_state = self._resolve_slot_state(event)
   555→            if slot_state is None:
   556→                return
   557→
   558→            # Calculate expected sync state
   559→            expected_in_sync = self._calculate_expected_sync(slot_state)
   560→
   561→            # Initial load: Set sync state without performing operations (prevents startup flapping)
   562→            if self._attr_is_on is None:
   563→                # Guard: Verify active state is valid
   564→                if slot_state.active_state not in (STATE_ON, STATE_OFF):
   565→                    _LOGGER.debug(
   566→                        "%s (%s): Active entity for %s slot %s has invalid state '%s'",
   567→                        self.config_entry.entry_id,
   568→                        self.config_entry.title,
   569→                        self.lock.lock.entity_id,
   570→                        self.slot_num,
   571→                        slot_state.active_state,
   572→                    )
   573→                    return
   574→
   575→                self._update_sync_state(expected_in_sync)
   576→                _LOGGER.debug(
   577→                    "%s (%s): Initial state loaded for %s slot %s, in_sync=%s",
   578→                    self.config_entry.entry_id,
   579→                    self.config_entry.title,
   580→                    self.lock.lock.entity_id,
   581→                    self.slot_num,
   582→                    expected_in_sync,
   583→                )
   584→                return
   585→
   586→            # Normal operation: Perform sync if needed
   587→            if not expected_in_sync:
   588→                self._update_sync_state(False)
   589→
   590→                # Perform sync operation
   591→                sync_performed = await self._perform_sync_operation(slot_state)
   592→
   593→                # Refresh coordinator to verify operation completed
   594→                # Rate limiting at provider level prevents excessive calls
   595→                if sync_performed:
   596→                    try:
   597→                        await self.coordinator.async_refresh()
   598→                    except UpdateFailed as err:
   599→                        _LOGGER.debug(
   600→                            "%s (%s): Coordinator refresh failed after sync for %s "
   601→                            "slot %s, scheduling retry: %s",
   602→                            self.config_entry.entry_id,
   603→                            self.config_entry.title,
   604→                            self.lock.lock.entity_id,
   605→                            self.slot_num,
   606→                            err,
   607→                        )
   608→                        self._schedule_retry()
   609→
   610→            elif not self._attr_is_on:
   611→                # Was out of sync, now in sync
   612→                self._update_sync_state(True)
   613→                self._cancel_retry()
   614→
   615→    async def async_added_to_hass(self) -> None:
   616→        """Handle entity added to hass."""
   617→        await BinarySensorEntity.async_added_to_hass(self)
   618→        await BaseLockCodeManagerCodeSlotPerLockEntity.async_added_to_hass(self)
   619→        await CoordinatorEntity.async_added_to_hass(self)
   620→
   621→        # Register cleanup for state tracking subscription
   622→        self.async_on_remove(self._cleanup_state_tracking)
   623→
   624→        self._setup_state_tracking()
   625→        await self._async_update_state()
   626→
   627→    @callback
   628→    def _cleanup_state_tracking(self) -> None:
   629→        """Clean up state tracking subscription if one exists."""
   630→        if self._state_tracking_unsub:
   631→            self._state_tracking_unsub()
   632→            self._state_tracking_unsub = None
   633→            self._tracking_all_states = False
   634→
   635→    @callback
   636→    def _setup_state_tracking(self) -> None:
   637→        """
   638→        Set up state change tracking for dependent entities.
   639→
   640→        If all entity IDs are available, tracks only those specific entities.
   641→        Otherwise, tracks all state changes until entities become available.
   642→        """
   643→        # Clean up existing subscription before setting up new one
   644→        self._cleanup_state_tracking()
   645→
   646→        if self._build_entity_id_map():
   647→            # All entities found - track only those
   648→            self._state_tracking_unsub = async_track_state_change_event(
   649→                self.hass, self._tracked_entity_ids, self._async_update_state
   650→            )
   651→            self._tracking_all_states = False
   652→        else:
   653→            # Some entities not yet registered - track all states temporarily
   654→            # _async_update_state has guards that return early if entities aren't ready
   655→            tracker = async_track_state_change_filtered(
   656→                self.hass,
   657→                TrackStates(True, set(), set()),
   658→                self._async_update_state,
   659→            )
   660→            self._state_tracking_unsub = tracker.async_remove
   661→            self._tracking_all_states = True
   662→            _LOGGER.debug(
   663→                "%s (%s): Waiting for dependent entities for %s slot %s, "
   664→                "tracking all state changes",
   665→                self.config_entry.entry_id,
   666→                self.config_entry.title,
   667→                self.lock.lock.entity_id,
   668→                self.slot_num,
   669→            )
   670→
   671→    async def _async_remove(self) -> None:
   672→        """Handle removal cleanup."""
   673→        self._cancel_retry()
   674→

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have the complete picture. Let me compile my findings:

## Summary of Findings

Based on my research, here are the answers to your four questions:

### 1. **Does LCM see ALL slots on the lock, or only the ones it manages?**

LCM **only sees and manages the configured slots**. Here's how:

**File:** `/Users/raman/projects/lock_code_manager/custom_components/lock_code_manager/providers/zwave_js.py` (lines 684-738)

In `async_get_usercodes()`:
- Lines 687-690: It retrieves the list of code slots **only** from the config entries that manage this lock:
  ```python
  code_slots = {
      int(code_slot)
      for entry in self.hass.config_entries.async_entries(DOMAIN)
      for code_slot in get_entry_data(entry, CONF_SLOTS, {})
      if self.lock.entity_id in get_entry_data(entry, CONF_LOCKS, [])
  }
  ```
- Lines 708-727: It then filters the cached Z-Wave values to only include these managed slots, and returns only those in the data dictionary.

**Push updates:** `/Users/raman/projects/lock_code_manager/custom_components/lock_code_manager/providers/zwave_js.py` (lines 272-389)

Push updates are also filtered to only process the specific code slots that are managed. Lines 295-301 show it returns early for slot 0 (which is metadata, not a user slot) and only processes updates relevant to the managed configuration.

**Polling:** The coordinator in `/Users/raman/projects/lock_code_manager/custom_components/lock_code_manager/coordinator.py` calls `async_get_usercodes()` which returns only managed slots. Line 43 shows polling is disabled when push is supported.

---

### 2. **Where are codes set on the lock?**

There are two paths:

**Path A - Configuration Entity Changes (Normal Flow):**
1. User changes PIN text entity or enabled switch
2. **File:** `/Users/raman/projects/lock_code_manager/custom_components/lock_code_manager/text.py` (line 99) or `/Users/raman/projects/lock_code_manager/custom_components/lock_code_manager/switch.py` (lines 77, 81)
   - Calls `self._update_config_entry(value)` which updates config entry data
3. **File:** `/Users/raman/projects/lock_code_manager/custom_components/lock_code_manager/__init__.py` (lines 405-645)
   - `async_update_listener` is triggered by config entry change
4. **File:** `/Users/raman/projects/lock_code_manager/custom_components/lock_code_manager/binary_sensor.py` (lines 502-542)
   - `LockCodeManagerCodeSlotInSyncEntity._perform_sync_operation()` compares config state vs coordinator data
   - Lines 509-512: Calls `await self.lock.async_internal_set_usercode()` when enabled and PIN differs
   - OR lines 521: Calls `await self.lock.async_internal_clear_usercode()` when disabled
5. **File:** `/Users/raman/projects/lock_code_manager/custom_components/lock_code_manager/providers/zwave_js.py` (lines 579-627)
   - `async_set_usercode()` method:
   - Line 617: Sets `self._set_in_progress_code_slot = code_slot` to track the ongoing set
   - Lines 612-613: Calls the Z-Wave service:
     ```python
     await self.async_call_service(
         ZWAVE_JS_DOMAIN, SERVICE_SET_LOCK_USERCODE, service_data
     )
     ```
   - Lines 622-625: Optimistic update - calls `self.coordinator.push_update({code_slot: usercode})` to avoid sync loops

**Path B - WebSocket Command (Direct Set):**
1. **File:** `/Users/raman/projects/lock_code_manager/custom_components/lock_code_manager/websocket.py` (lines 1100-1149)
   - `set_lock_usercode()` function (lines 1133-1140)
   - Directly calls `await lock.async_internal_set_usercode()` or `await lock.async_internal_clear_usercode()`

---

### 3. **What does the duplicate code event handler from PR #899 do?**

**File:** `/Users/raman/projects/lock_code_manager/custom_components/lock_code_manager/providers/zwave_js.py` (lines 478-540)

`_async_handle_duplicate_code()` flow:

1. **Event Detection** (lines 451-462): When Z-Wave event `NEW_USER_CODE_NOT_ADDED_DUE_TO_DUPLICATE_CODE` (AccessControl notification event 15) arrives:
   - Line 453: Only triggers if `self._set_in_progress_code_slot` is not None (LCM initiated the set)
   - Line 454: Checks if the event's userId matches 0 or the in-progress slot
   - Lines 458-462: Creates async task to handle it

2. **Handler Actions** (lines 479-540):
   - Line 482: Clears the `_set_in_progress_code_slot` tracking
   - Lines 486-498: Finds the config entry managing this lock/slot combination
   - Lines 501-508: Gets the "enabled" switch entity ID for this slot
   - Lines 510-516: **Turns off the switch** (`SERVICE_TURN_OFF`) to disable the slot (line 511)
   - Lines 518-540: Creates a persistent notification to the user with message:
     ```
     "Lock {lock_entity_id} rejected the code for slot {code_slot} (config entry {title}) 
      because it duplicates a code in another slot. Slot {code_slot} has been disabled. 
      Please set a unique code and re-enable slot {code_slot}"
     ```

---

### 4. **Is there any existing duplicate code detection before sending to the lock?**

**No, there is NO pre-flight duplicate code detection.** The system is reactive, not preventive:

1. **File:** `/Users/raman/projects/lock_code_manager/custom_components/lock_code_manager/providers/zwave_js.py` (lines 594-620)
   - `async_set_usercode()` only checks if the code is **already set to the same value** (lines 603-609) to avoid unnecessary network calls
   - No validation of whether the code duplicates another slot's code

2. The system relies on:
   - The **lock's native duplicate detection** (which sends the Z-Wave notification)
   - The **event handler (PR #899)** to react to that notification
   - User re-enabling and setting a unique code

3. **File:** `/Users/raman/projects/lock_code_manager/custom_components/lock_code_manager/binary_sensor.py` (lines 587-608)
   - The sync mechanism just calls the set operation and then refreshes coordinator data
   - No pre-check for duplicates

This is a lock-level concern - the Z-Wave lock firmware itself detects and rejects duplicates, and LCM handles the rejection gracefully by disabling the slot and notifying the user.

1. **Does LCM see ALL slots on the lock, or only the ones it manages?** Look at:

2. **Where are codes set on the lock?** Find the exact method that calls the Z-Wave service to set a usercode. I need to understand the flow from "LCM decides to set a code" to "Z-Wave service call happens".

3. **What does the duplicate code event handler from PR #899 do?** Find the handler for event 15 / `NEW_USER_CODE_NOT_ADDED_DUE_TO_DUPLICATE_CODE` and understand its full flow.

4. **Is there any existing duplicate code detection before sending to the lock?**

For each, give me the exact file paths and line numbers. Read the actual method bodies — don't just look at signatures. This is research only, no code changes.

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have the complete picture. Let me compile my findings:

## Summary of Findings

Based on my research, here are the answers to your four questions:

### 1. **Does LCM see ALL slots on the lock, or only the ones it manages?**

LCM **only sees and manages the configured slots**. Here's how:

**File:** `/Users/raman/projects/lock_code_manager/custom_components/lock_code_manager/providers/zwave_js.py` (lines 684-738)

In `async_get_usercodes()`:
- Lines 687-690: It retrieves the list of code slots **only** from the config entries that manage this lock:
  ```python
  code_slots = {
      int(code_slot)
      for entry in self.hass.config_entries.async_entries(DOMAIN)
      for code_slot in get_entry_data(entry, CONF_SLOTS, {})
      if self.lock.entity_id in get_entry_data(entry, CONF_LOCKS, [])
  }
  ```
- Lines 708-727: It then filters the cached Z-Wave values to only include these managed slots, and returns only those in the data dictionary.

**Push updates:** `/Users/raman/projects/lock_code_manager/custom_components/lock_code_manager/providers/zwave_js.py` (lines 272-389)

Push updates are also filtered to only process the specific code slots that are managed. Lines 295-301 show it returns early for slot 0 (which is metadata, not a user slot) and only processes updates relevant to the managed configuration.

**Polling:** The coordinator in `/Users/raman/projects/lock_code_manager/custom_components/lock_code_manager/coordinator.py` calls `async_get_usercodes()` which returns only managed slots. Line 43 shows polling is disabled when push is supported.

---

### 2. **Where are codes set on the lock?**

There are two paths:

**Path A - Configuration Entity Changes (Normal Flow):**
1. User changes PIN text entity or enabled switch
2. **File:** `/Users/raman/projects/lock_code_manager/custom_components/lock_code_manager/text.py` (line 99) or `/Users/raman/projects/lock_code_manager/custom_components/lock_code_manager/switch.py` (lines 77, 81)
   - Calls `self._update_config_entry(value)` which updates config entry data
3. **File:** `/Users/raman/projects/lock_code_manager/custom_components/lock_code_manager/__init__.py` (lines 405-645)
   - `async_update_listener` is triggered by config entry change
4. **File:** `/Users/raman/projects/lock_code_manager/custom_components/lock_code_manager/binary_sensor.py` (lines 502-542)
   - `LockCodeManagerCodeSlotInSyncEntity._perform_sync_operation()` compares config state vs coordinator data
   - Lines 509-512: Calls `await self.lock.async_internal_set_usercode()` when enabled and PIN differs
   - OR lines 521: Calls `await self.lock.async_internal_clear_usercode()` when disabled
5. **File:** `/Users/raman/projects/lock_code_manager/custom_components/lock_code_manager/providers/zwave_js.py` (lines 579-627)
   - `async_set_usercode()` method:
   - Line 617: Sets `self._set_in_progress_code_slot = code_slot` to track the ongoing set
   - Lines 612-613: Calls the Z-Wave service:
     ```python
     await self.async_call_service(
         ZWAVE_JS_DOMAIN, SERVICE_SET_LOCK_USERCODE, service_data
     )
     ```
   - Lines 622-625: Optimistic update - calls `self.coordinator.push_update({code_slot: usercode})` to avoid sync loops

**Path B - WebSocket Command (Direct Set):**
1. **File:** `/Users/raman/projects/lock_code_manager/custom_components/lock_code_manager/websocket.py` (lines 1100-1149)
   - `set_lock_usercode()` function (lines 1133-1140)
   - Directly calls `await lock.async_internal_set_usercode()` or `await lock.async_internal_clear_usercode()`

---

### 3. **What does the duplicate code event handler from PR #899 do?**

**File:** `/Users/raman/projects/lock_code_manager/custom_components/lock_code_manager/providers/zwave_js.py` (lines 478-540)

`_async_handle_duplicate_code()` flow:

1. **Event Detection** (lines 451-462): When Z-Wave event `NEW_USER_CODE_NOT_ADDED_DUE_TO_DUPLICATE_CODE` (AccessControl notification event 15) arrives:
   - Line 453: Only triggers if `self._set_in_progress_code_slot` is not None (LCM initiated the set)
   - Line 454: Checks if the event's userId matches 0 or the in-progress slot
   - Lines 458-462: Creates async task to handle it

2. **Handler Actions** (lines 479-540):
   - Line 482: Clears the `_set_in_progress_code_slot` tracking
   - Lines 486-498: Finds the config entry managing this lock/slot combination
   - Lines 501-508: Gets the "enabled" switch entity ID for this slot
   - Lines 510-516: **Turns off the switch** (`SERVICE_TURN_OFF`) to disable the slot (line 511)
   - Lines 518-540: Creates a persistent notification to the user with message:
     ```
     "Lock {lock_entity_id} rejected the code for slot {code_slot} (config entry {title}) 
      because it duplicates a code in another slot. Slot {code_slot} has been disabled. 
      Please set a unique code and re-enable slot {code_slot}"
     ```

---

### 4. **Is there any existing duplicate code detection before sending to the lock?**

**No, there is NO pre-flight duplicate code detection.** The system is reactive, not preventive:

1. **File:** `/Users/raman/projects/lock_code_manager/custom_components/lock_code_manager/providers/zwave_js.py` (lines 594-620)
   - `async_set_usercode()` only checks if the code is **already set to the same value** (lines 603-609) to avoid unnecessary network calls
   - No validation of whether the code duplicates another slot's code

2. The system relies on:
   - The **lock's native duplicate detection** (which sends the Z-Wave notification)
   - The **event handler (PR #899)** to react to that notification
   - User re-enabling and setting a unique code

3. **File:** `/Users/raman/projects/lock_code_manager/custom_components/lock_code_manager/binary_sensor.py` (lines 587-608)
   - The sync mechanism just calls the set operation and then refreshes coordinator data
   - No pre-check for duplicates

This is a lock-level concern - the Z-Wave lock firmware itself detects and rejects duplicates, and LCM handles the rejection gracefully by disabling the slot and notifying the user.

Here's what I found:

**LCM only sees managed slots.** Both push updates and polling are filtered to only the code slots configured in LCM config entries. `async_get_usercodes()` in `zwave_js.py:687-690` explicitly builds the slot set from config entries:

```python
code_slots = {
    int(code_slot)
    for entry in self.hass.config_entries.async_entries(DOMAIN)
    for code_slot in get_entry_data(entry, CONF_SLOTS, {})
    if self.lock.entity_id in get_entry_data(entry, CONF_LOCKS, [])
}
```

Push updates are similarly scoped. So **LCM is blind to codes in unmanaged slots.**

**There is zero pre-flight duplicate detection.** `async_set_usercode()` only checks if the code is already set to the same value (to skip no-op calls). The entire duplicate handling is reactive via the Z-Wave event 15 handler from PR #899.

### Challenging your assertion

**"If we fix it in house we don't even need the event handling"** — this doesn't hold, for two reasons:

1. **Unmanaged slots are invisible.** The user's report confirms this: they had codes in slots outside LCM's range. Even if we read ALL managed slots and check for duplicates among them, we'd still miss conflicts with unmanaged slots. To truly handle this in-house, we'd need to change `async_get_usercodes()` to read **every slot on the lock**, not just managed ones. That's a bigger change — more network traffic, potentially slow on locks with many slots, and some locks may not support enumerating all slots efficiently.

2. **TOCTOU race.** Even if we read all slots right before setting, someone could set a code at the keypad between our read and our write. The lock's native rejection + event handler is the only guaranteed backstop.

### What we CAN do in-house

We can add a **pre-flight check across managed slots** before calling the Z-Wave set service. Since LCM knows all the PINs it manages (they're in config entry data), it can check if a PIN being set on slot N already exists in any other managed slot on the same lock. This would:

- Catch the most common case (user accidentally sets the same PIN in two LCM slots)
- Give an immediate, clear error instead of waiting for the async Z-Wave rejection
- Avoid the round-trip to the lock entirely

But the **event handler from #899 is still needed** as a safety net for unmanaged-slot conflicts and race conditions. The two approaches are complementary, not alternatives.

Want me to look into implementing the pre-flight managed-slot duplicate check?

You're right. `_get_usercodes_from_cache()` calls `get_usercodes(self.node)` which returns **all** slots from the Z-Wave cache, and the `for slot in slots` loop at line 718 iterates over all of them — not filtered by `code_slots`. So `async_get_usercodes()` already returns every slot the lock has, managed or not.

Now let me check what the coordinator does with that data and how the UI shows unmanaged codes.

content.text:
You're right — I was wrong earlier. `async_get_usercodes()` returns **all** slots from the Z-Wave cache, not just managed ones. The `code_slots` filter is only used to decide whether a hard refresh is needed. The coordinator and UI already have full visibility into every slot on the lock, including unmanaged ones.

That changes the calculus significantly. Since we already have the complete picture of every code on the lock:

1. **We can do a full pre-flight duplicate check** — before setting a code, scan all slots (managed and unmanaged) in the coordinator data for the same PIN
2. **The event handler from #899 becomes a redundant safety net** — still worth keeping for edge cases (race with physical keypad entry), but the pre-flight check would catch the vast majority of cases
3. **We can give much better error messages** — "Slot 3 has the same PIN as unmanaged slot 12" vs the current generic "lock rejected due to duplicate"

The fix would go in `async_set_usercode()` or `_perform_sync_operation()` — before calling the Z-Wave set service, check the coordinator's current data for any other slot with the same PIN value. Want me to plan this out?

message.content: lets plan this out, but include in the plan a review of places where we imply that we only actively manage a subset of codes and make sure it's more clear. We may want to consider adding to the module docstring with an overarching view of what gets polled, what gets hard refreshed, what gets pushed, etc. Ideally there is a better way to do this than to make a giant docstring but consider that idea as you think through this secondary part of the plan

Base directory for this skill: /Users/raman/.claude/plugins/cache/superpowers-marketplace/superpowers/4.3.0/skills/brainstorming

# Brainstorming Ideas Into Designs

## Overview

Help turn ideas into fully formed designs and specs through natural collaborative dialogue.

Start by understanding the current project context, then ask questions one at a time to refine the idea. Once you understand what you're building, present the design and get user approval.

<HARD-GATE>
Do NOT invoke any implementation skill, write any code, scaffold any project, or take any implementation action until you have presented a design and the user has approved it. This applies to EVERY project regardless of perceived simplicity.
</HARD-GATE>

## Anti-Pattern: "This Is Too Simple To Need A Design"

Every project goes through this process. A todo list, a single-function utility, a config change — all of them. "Simple" projects are where unexamined assumptions cause the most wasted work. The design can be short (a few sentences for truly simple projects), but you MUST present it and get approval.

## Checklist

You MUST create a task for each of these items and complete them in order:

1. **Explore project context** — check files, docs, recent commits
2. **Ask clarifying questions** — one at a time, understand purpose/constraints/success criteria
3. **Propose 2-3 approaches** — with trade-offs and your recommendation
4. **Present design** — in sections scaled to their complexity, get user approval after each section
5. **Write design doc** — save to `docs/plans/YYYY-MM-DD-<topic>-design.md` and commit
6. **Transition to implementation** — invoke writing-plans skill to create implementation plan

## Process Flow

```dot
digraph brainstorming {
    "Explore project context" [shape=box];
    "Ask clarifying questions" [shape=box];
    "Propose 2-3 approaches" [shape=box];
    "Present design sections" [shape=box];
    "User approves design?" [shape=diamond];
    "Write design doc" [shape=box];
    "Invoke writing-plans skill" [shape=doublecircle];

    "Explore project context" -> "Ask clarifying questions";
    "Ask clarifying questions" -> "Propose 2-3 approaches";
    "Propose 2-3 approaches" -> "Present design sections";
    "Present design sections" -> "User approves design?";
    "User approves design?" -> "Present design sections" [label="no, revise"];
    "User approves design?" -> "Write design doc" [label="yes"];
    "Write design doc" -> "Invoke writing-plans skill";
}
```

**The terminal state is invoking writing-plans.** Do NOT invoke frontend-design, mcp-builder, or any other implementation skill. The ONLY skill you invoke after brainstorming is writing-plans.

## The Process

**Understanding the idea:**
- Check out the current project state first (files, docs, recent commits)
- Ask questions one at a time to refine the idea
- Prefer multiple choice questions when possible, but open-ended is fine too
- Only one question per message - if a topic needs more exploration, break it into multiple questions
- Focus on understanding: purpose, constraints, success criteria

**Exploring approaches:**
- Propose 2-3 different approaches with trade-offs
- Present options conversationally with your recommendation and reasoning
- Lead with your recommended option and explain why

**Presenting the design:**
- Once you believe you understand what you're building, present the design
- Scale each section to its complexity: a few sentences if straightforward, up to 200-300 words if nuanced
- Ask after each section whether it looks right so far
- Cover: architecture, components, data flow, error handling, testing
- Be ready to go back and clarify if something doesn't make sense

## After the Design

**Documentation:**
- Write the validated design to `docs/plans/YYYY-MM-DD-<topic>-design.md`
- Use elements-of-style:writing-clearly-and-concisely skill if available
- Commit the design document to git

**Implementation:**
- Invoke the writing-plans skill to create a detailed implementation plan
- Do NOT invoke any other skill. writing-plans is the next step.

## Key Principles

- **One question at a time** - Don't overwhelm with multiple questions
- **Multiple choice preferred** - Easier to answer than open-ended when possible
- **YAGNI ruthlessly** - Remove unnecessary features from all designs
- **Explore alternatives** - Always propose 2-3 approaches before settling
- **Incremental validation** - Present design, get approval before moving on
- **Be flexible** - Go back and clarify when something doesn't make sense

{"result":"# Lock Code Manager - Project Overview

## Purpose
Home Assistant custom integration for managing lock usercodes across multiple locks. Reduces entity/automation sprawl by handling logic internally.

## Tech Stack
- **Backend**: Python 3.13, Home Assistant custom component
- **Frontend**: TypeScript, Lit, Rollup
- **Testing**: pytest + pytest-homeassistant-custom-component (Python), vitest (TypeScript)
- **Linting**: Ruff (Python), ESLint (TypeScript), pre-commit hooks
- **Package management**: uv (Python), yarn (TypeScript)

## Architecture
- **Providers** (`providers/`): Plugin-based lock integration support (_base.py, zwave_js.py, virtual.py)
- **Coordinator** (`coordinator.py`): DataUpdateCoordinator per lock, supports push updates
- **Config Flow** (`config_flow.py`): Multi-step: select locks → configure slots
- **Entities**: binary_sensor, sensor, text, number, switch, event
- **Frontend**: Lit components (slot card, lock codes card), Lovelace strategies

## Key External Repos (all in parent directory `../`)
- `home-assistant/` - HA core
- `zwave-js-server-python/` - Python Z-Wave JS client library
- `zwave-js-server/` - Z-Wave JS server
- `lock_code_manager.wiki/` - GitHub wiki
- `frontend/` - HA frontend
"}

{"result":"# Code Style & Conventions

## Python
- **Linter/Formatter**: Ruff (line length: 88)
- **Import order**: future → stdlib → third-party → homeassistant → first-party → local
- **Type annotations**: Use PEP 604 `X | None` / `X | Y` syntax and lowercase builtins (`dict`, `list`). UP006/UP007 are enforced.
- **Docstrings**: Google style, required for all public functions
- **Async**: Prefer async/await; use `hass.async_add_executor_job()` for sync wrappers
- **ruff ignores**: D202, D203, D212, D213, E501, PLE1205, various PLR/PLW rules (all annotated with rationale)

## TypeScript
- Uses ESLint
- Lit framework for web components

## Design Patterns
- **Dual storage**: Config entries use both `data` and `options` fields
- **Internal methods**: Providers expose `async_internal_*` methods with asyncio locks
- **Rate limiting**: 2-second minimum delay between lock operations
- **Dispatcher signals**: For dynamic entity management
- **Optimistic updates**: For providers with async value caches (e.g., Z-Wave JS)
"}

"""Lock Code Manager Coordinators."""

from __future__ import annotations

from collections.abc import Callable
from datetime import datetime
import logging
from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any

from homeassistant.core import HomeAssistant, callback
from homeassistant.helpers.event import async_track_time_interval
from homeassistant.helpers.update_coordinator import DataUpdateCoordinator, UpdateFailed

from .const import DOMAIN
from .exceptions import LockCodeManagerError

if TYPE_CHECKING:
    from .providers import BaseLock

_LOGGER = logging.getLogger(__name__)


class LockUsercodeUpdateCoordinator(DataUpdateCoordinator[dict[int, int | str]]):
    """Class to manage usercode updates."""

    def __init__(self, hass: HomeAssistant, lock: BaseLock, config_entry: Any) -> None:
        """Initialize the usercode update coordinator."""
        self._lock = lock

{"result":"[{\"name_path\": \"async_setup_entry\", \"kind\": \"Function\", \"relative_path\": \"custom_components/lock_code_manager/binary_sensor.py\", \"body_location\": {\"start_line\": 61, \"end_line\": 111}, \"body\": \"async def async_setup_entry(\\n    hass: HomeAssistant,\\n    config_entry: LockCodeManagerConfigEntry,\\n    async_add_entities: AddEntitiesCallback,\\n) -> bool:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"Set up config entry.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n\\n    @callback\\n    def add_pin_active_entity(slot_num: int, ent_reg: er.EntityRegistry) -> None:\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"Add active binary sensor entities for slot.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        async_add_entities(\\n            [\\n                LockCodeManagerActiveEntity(\\n                    hass, ent_reg, config_entry, slot_num, ATTR_ACTIVE\\n                )\\n            ],\\n            True,\\n        )\\n\\n    @callback\\n    def add_code_slot_entities(\\n        lock: BaseLock, slot_num: int, ent_reg: er.EntityRegistry\\n    ):\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"Add code slot sensor entities for slot.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        coordinator = lock.coordinator\\n        if coordinator is None:\\n            _LOGGER.warning(\\n                \\\"%s (%s): Coordinator missing for lock %s when adding slot %s entities\\\",\\n                config_entry.entry_id,\\n                config_entry.title,\\n                lock.lock.entity_id,\\n                slot_num,\\n            )\\n            return\\n        async_add_entities(\\n            [\\n                LockCodeManagerCodeSlotInSyncEntity(\\n                    hass, ent_reg, config_entry, coordinator, lock, slot_num\\n                )\\n            ],\\n            True,\\n        )\\n\\n    callbacks = config_entry.runtime_data.callbacks\\n    config_entry.async_on_unload(\\n        callbacks.register_standard_adder(add_pin_active_entity)\\n    )\\n    config_entry.async_on_unload(\\n        callbacks.register_lock_slot_adder(add_code_slot_entities)\\n    )\\n    return True\"}]"}

{"result":"[{\"name_path\": \"LockCodeManagerCodeSlotInSyncEntity/_resolve_slot_state\", \"kind\": \"Method\", \"relative_path\": \"custom_components/lock_code_manager/binary_sensor.py\", \"body_location\": {\"start_line\": 447, \"end_line\": 499}, \"body\": \"def _resolve_slot_state(\\n        self, event: Event[EventStateChangedData] | None\\n    ) -> _SlotState | None:\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"Resolve slot state while applying shared guards.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        entity_id = event.data[\\\"entity_id\\\"] if event else None\\n        to_state = event.data[\\\"new_state\\\"] if event else None\\n\\n        if not self.coordinator.last_update_success and not self._retry_active:\\n            return None\\n\\n        if not self._build_entity_id_map():\\n            return None\\n\\n        # NOTE: We intentionally don't call _update_state_tracking_filter() here.\\n        # While it would be nice to switch from tracking all states to only specific\\n        # entities, modifying subscriptions from within a callback causes timing issues.\\n        # The performance impact of tracking all states is minimal since this method\\n        # has early-return guards that skip processing for irrelevant entities.\\n\\n        if self._attr_is_on is None and int(self.slot_num) not in self.coordinator.data:\\n            _LOGGER.debug(\\n                \\\"%s (%s): Slot %s not yet in coordinator data, skipping\\\",\\n                self.config_entry.entry_id,\\n                self.config_entry.title,\\n                self.slot_num,\\n            )\\n            return None\\n\\n        if entity_id is not None and entity_id not in self._tracked_entity_ids:\\n            return None\\n\\n        if to_state and to_state.state in (STATE_UNAVAILABLE, STATE_UNKNOWN):\\n            return None\\n\\n        if not self._ensure_entities_ready():\\n            return None\\n\\n        active_state = self._get_entity_state(ATTR_ACTIVE)\\n        pin_state = self._get_entity_state(CONF_PIN)\\n        name_state = self._get_entity_state(CONF_NAME)\\n        code_state = self._get_entity_state(ATTR_CODE)\\n\\n        if active_state is None or pin_state is None or code_state is None:\\n            return None\\n\\n        coordinator_code = self.coordinator.data.get(int(self.slot_num))\\n        return self._SlotState(\\n            active_state=active_state,\\n            pin_state=pin_state,\\n            name_state=name_state,\\n            code_state=code_state,\\n            coordinator_code=coordinator_code,\\n        )\"}]"}
